subject: Cloud Computing
title: NoSQL in the Cloud
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on NoSQL in the Cloud.

hook.text: Amazon built DynamoDB because no existing database could handle Black Friday traffic. It auto-scales to any load.

buildup.text: NoSQL databases trade rigid structure for flexibility. Key-value, document, column, and graph each fit different data.

discovery.text: DynamoDB, Cosmos DB, and Firestore scale horizontally. Add more traffic and they grow automatically.

twist.text: NoSQL doesn't mean no structure. You still need to design your data model carefully — mistakes are expensive to fix.

climax.text: NoSQL dominates gaming, IoT, and real-time apps where speed and scale matter more than complex queries.

punchline.text: NoSQL scales where relational can't. But structure still matters.

quiz.question: What type of applications benefit most from NoSQL databases?
options: Applications requiring massive scale and speed like gaming and IoT
Applications with complex multi-table joins and transactions
Small personal websites with fewer than 100 users
